mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals robust consumer spending that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 40 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, extreme monetary accommodation fuels liquidity-driven asset price inflation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns during sub-0.5 percent rate environments show no positive alpha relative to the risk-free rate over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration, which suppresses long-term growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE produces a statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect immediate construction sector demand and consumer confidence, which transmit to equity markets as positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts data shows zero predictive power for 5-day equity returns, with an information coefficient consistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105 during a post-shock recovery, rapid capacity utilization drives earnings revisions that generate positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO crosses 105 during recovery phases, invalidating the earnings revision channel.
